title: Year 7 Arts: Kaitiakitanga in Action - Comprehensive Learning Journey
subject: Arts
culturalContext: Creating comprehensive English unit plans that combine traditional pūrākau with contemporary narratives connected to our local community and cultural heritage through integrated, culturally-responsive learning experiences.
learningObjectives: Develop comprehensive understanding of arts concepts through integrated, culturally-responsive learning
Apply traditional knowledge and modern methods to arts challenges
Connect learning to local environment, community, and cultural heritage
Demonstrate respect for Māori and Pacific perspectives and values
Use Te Reo Māori appropriately in arts contexts
activities: Traditional knowledge workshop and arts integration
Community interview project on arts applications
Cultural artifact study through arts lens
resources: Te Reo Māori language resources and dictionaries

tasks: Formative: Regular check-ins, observations, and feedback throughout the unit
Formative: Peer collaboration and self-reflection on learning progress
Summative: Comprehensive project demonstrating understanding, cultural connection, and community application
Summative: Portfolio of work showing growth in knowledge, skills, and cultural understanding

nzcAlignment: Te Tiriti o Waitangi: Cultural narratives and perspectives
unitType: Integrated Literacy and Cultural Storytelling Journey

unitStructure.title: Whakawhanaungatanga: Building Learning Community
unitStructure.description: Begin with karakia and establish cultural protocols. Students explore their prior knowledge, experiences, and personal connections to the unit topic. Set clear expectations and cultural guidelines.

unitStructure.title: Cultural Context and Foundation Learning
unitStructure.description: Students will engage in a comprehensive literacy journey that explores traditional Māori storytelling, contemporary narratives, and community voice through interconnected lessons and authentic assessments.

unitStructure.title: Integrated Learning and Application
unitStructure.description: Students engage in integrated learning experiences that connect arts concepts to real-world applications, community needs, and cultural values through collaborative projects and authentic assessments.
materials: Te Reo Māori language resources and dictionaries

unitStructure.title: Synthesis and Cultural Sharing
unitStructure.description: Students synthesize their learning through comprehensive projects, presentations, and cultural sharing that demonstrate both academic achievement and cultural understanding while contributing to community knowledge.

differentiation.support: Provide additional scaffolding, visual aids, and one-on-one support for students who need extra help
differentiation.extension: Offer advanced challenges, independent research opportunities, and leadership roles for students who excel
differentiation.cultural: Ensure all students can connect to their own cultural backgrounds while learning about Māori perspectives

crossCurricularConnections.teReoMāori: Integration of Te Reo Māori vocabulary and cultural concepts throughout the unit
crossCurricularConnections.socialStudies: Connection to local history, community, and cultural context
crossCurricularConnections.science: Environmental and scientific connections where applicable
crossCurricularConnections.technology: Use of appropriate technology tools and digital resources
crossCurricularConnections.arts: Creative expression and cultural art forms where relevant

communityConnections.localExperts: Invitation of local experts, elders, and community members to share knowledge
communityConnections.fieldTrips: Visits to relevant local sites, cultural centers, and community facilities
communityConnections.communityProjects: Opportunities for students to contribute to community initiatives
communityConnections.culturalProtocols: Respectful engagement with cultural protocols and tikanga
